# Code review iteration

Scope: code-quality findings on `main.go` + `internal/**` — best-practice fixes,
hidden bugs, and refactors. Matched against `reviews/20260517T103045Z.md` so
nothing is duplicated.

Spec-compliance discrepancies that this file originally carried have been moved
to `reports/20260601T084925Z.md` (the place for plan-conformance gaps, mirroring
`reports/20260517T101938Z.md`). Only fresh code-review items remain below.

Severity: **Crit** / **High** / **Med** / **Low** / **Nit**.

## Summary

| Ref            | Gap                                                               | Sev |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- | --- |
| `common.go:32` | [[#1. A bad --timeout value exits 1 instead of the usage code 2]] | Low |
| `auth.go:75`   | [[#2. auth status logs the proxy line twice]]                     | Nit |
| `auth.go:21`   | [[#3. Login emits the authorized marker through slog.Default]]    | Nit |

## 1. A bad --timeout value exits 1 instead of the usage code 2

`internal/command/telegram/common.go:31-35`:

```go
timeout, err := time.ParseDuration(opt.timeout)
if err != nil {
    return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("parse --timeout: %w", err)
}
```

A malformed flag value (`--timeout=30`, `--timeout=abc`) is a user-input error,
which `main.go`'s contract maps to exit code `2`. But this returns a plain
`fmt.Errorf`, so it falls through to the default `exitcode.Fail` (`1`) — the same
bucket as transport/auth failures. Sibling parsers in the same file already do
the right thing: `parseRFC3339` errors are wrapped in `usageErr(...)` at the call
sites (`fetch.go:132-139`), and `proxy` errors go through `usageErr` too
(`common.go:53`).

**Fix.** Wrap the parse error with `usageErr(...)` (or return
`exitcode.New(exitcode.Usage, err)`), the way `--from`/`--to` and proxy parsing
already do.

## 2. auth status logs the proxy line twice

`internal/command/telegram/auth.go:72-79` logs the proxy descriptor, then —
when the session file exists — calls `newClient`, which logs the identical line
again at `common.go:67-71`. With no session the line prints once; with a session
it prints twice. Harmless, but noisy for a diagnostic command.

**Fix.** Drop the proxy log from the status command and rely on the one in
`newClient`, or skip `newClient`'s log when called from `status`.

## 3. Login emits the authorized marker through slog.Default

`reviews/20260517T103045Z.md` §3.1 flagged `fetch.go` and `rate.go` reaching
into `slog.Default()` instead of the injected logger. `internal/telegram/auth.go:21`
is a third site not listed there:

```go
slog.Default().Info("authorized", ...)
```

So the `auth login` lifecycle marker only surfaces because `log.Setup` mutates
the global default (`log.go:54`) — the same coupling §3.1 wants gone. Folding it
into the §3.1 fix (thread the logger via `ctx`/`log.FromContext`) closes all
three sites at once.
